content:
McKee argues that a character is not defined by their background, dialogue, or narration—they are defined by their choices when faced with obstacles.

🔥 How Whiplash Follows This Principle
Andrew’s character arc is built entirely around decisions under extreme pressure:
1. First Test (Practice Scene) → Fletcher walks in on Andrew practicing and coldly leaves after he fails to impress him.
• Decision: Andrew doubles down and practices harder.
• Consequence: He gets a second chance.
2. Second Test (First Studio Band Scene – “Not My Tempo”) → Fletcher humiliates Andrew, throwing a chair at him.
• Decision: Andrew continues despite bleeding hands.
• Consequence: He believes suffering = success, reinforcing his obsession.
3. Third Test (Car Crash Performance) → After an accident, Andrew drags himself onstage, covered in blood to play.
• Decision: He prioritizes Fletcher’s approval over his own well-being.
• Consequence: He is expelled.
context: From document: 1.1.1. | The Story Problem | subheading — chunk 4 (chunk 4/8)
